title: Versioning — Version Chain, Waveform Editor, Waveform Player, Freeze
problem: A raw recording needs to evolve from v1 (messy, includes setup and dead air) to vFinal (clean, trimmed) through collaborative edits — with full history and a clear end state (freeze). The band needs to listen to any version, compare versions, and hear exactly what a cut sounds like before committing.
analysis:
Two dedicated components with separate concerns:

**WaveformPlayer** (clip detail page) — pure playback. Receives activeCuts from parent (ClipDetailClient). Visualises cuts as blue non-draggable regions (pointerEvents: none on regions so they don't block click-to-seek). Skip-cuts during playback via timeupdate handler. H:MM:SS clock + virtual duration display. Stamp chips are tappable — each seeks to that timestamp. Big 64px play button. finish event resets cursor to start (ws.setTime(0)) so the amber cursor doesn't pin at the right edge after playback ends.

**WaveformEditor** (edit page, always edit mode) — cut creation only. Custom pointer overlay (absolute inset-0, touch-none, z-20 inside WaveformCanvas) handles tap-to-seek and drag-to-create. Cut bands are pure CSS divs (no RegionsPlugin). useCutInteraction manages the drag state machine; edge drag resizes existing cuts. Trim shortcut (pre-seeds start+end cuts). Split mode (mark a time position, confirm → creates TWO new clips: Part A (0→splitMs) and Part B (splitMs→end); original clip left completely unchanged as a reference; navigates to session page showing all three clips). Submit → new version in Postgres, router.back().

**Non-destructive split with immediate Drive files**: original clip is never mutated. Splitting Song X into Part A and Part B: the split API downloads X's source once, runs FFmpeg twice in parallel to produce two trimmed files (A: 0→splitMs, B: splitMs→end), uploads both to Drive with their own names, and creates two new Clip rows with their own driveFileId + correct sourceDurationMs + empty v1 cutMarks (the trimmed file is their raw). The original clip is untouched. After confirming that A and B are good, X's source can be deleted from Drive to free space. No provenance UI is shown on split children (no scissors icon, no 'Split into' card) — they are fully independent songs from the user's perspective. sourceClipId exists in the DB for data integrity but is not surfaced in the UI.

**ClipDetailClient** — owns selectedVersionId state. Feeds activeCuts to WaveformPlayer. Feeds activeVersionId to CollaborationSection for version-aware voting. Version list as large tappable cards. Clicking active version keeps it selected (no deselect/null state). Edit link to /edit page.

Versions are metadata only (cutMarks JSON) pointing at the same source audio. Source audio never mutated. Linear chain. localStorage safety net for in-progress edits.
successCriteria:
- Version list shows full chain: v number, description, result duration, cut count
- Selecting a version in the detail page updates the waveform player to skip that version's cuts
- Click-to-seek works on the waveform — clicking anywhere moves the playhead, play starts from that position
- Stamp chips in the player are tappable and seek to that timestamp
- After playback ends, cursor resets to start (no amber pin at right edge)
- Submit button is always active — description is optional
- Any member can create a version from any existing version
- Trim shortcut pre-seeds start+end cut marks
- Split: marks a time position → FFmpeg trims two independent Drive files (Part A: 0→split, Part B: split→end) → creates two new clips each with their own driveFileId and correct sourceDurationMs → original clip unchanged → navigates to session page showing all three clips
- Split children show correct durations (4 min / 5 min) in the clip list — not the full original duration
- Split children are fully independent songs with no visible lineage to the original (no scissors icon, no provenance card)
- Drag on the edit waveform creates a cut region
- Freeze locks the version and triggers render (see feat-render)
- In-progress edits survive browser crash via localStorage draft
- Draft banner shown on editor reopen with Resume / Dismiss
implementation:
1. components/clips/WaveformPlayer.tsx: pure player component. Props: clipId, sourceDurationMs, activeCuts[], stamps[]. Regions rendered with pointerEvents:none (display only, don't intercept seek clicks). timeupdate handler: position tracking (currentTimeMsRef + setCurrentTimeMs) runs unconditionally — isPlaying() guard wraps only the cut-skip section so currentTimeMs updates correctly even while paused (needed for re-seek before play). finish event: ws.setTime(0) resets cursor to start. Stamp chips are <button> elements that call ws.setTime(). No enableDragSelection.
2. components/clips/WaveformEditor.tsx: edit-only component. Props: clipId, sourceDurationMs, frozenVersionId, initialVersions, stamps, sessionHref. Custom pointer overlay (onPointerDown/Move/Up + setPointerCapture, touch-action:none) handles tap-to-seek + drag-to-create. Trim/Clear all/Split tool buttons. Split confirmation sheet updated to reflect Drive file creation ('Each part gets its own audio file in Drive. Takes a few seconds — hang tight.'). Split navigates to sessionHref after success. Auto-saves draft on every cut change. router.back() after version submit. No provenance UI (splitInto prop removed, scissors icon removed from 'Start from' version buttons).
3. components/clips/ClipDetailClient.tsx: coordinator. Owns selectedVersionId state (auto-selects latest, no deselect). Passes activeCuts to WaveformPlayer. Passes activeVersionId to CollaborationSection. Actions section hidden when !canEdit && !frozen.
4. app/(auth)/.../clips/[clipId]/page.tsx: server component — loads clip, versions, stamps, votes, comments, membership. Renders ClipDetailClient.
5. app/(auth)/.../clips/[clipId]/edit/page.tsx: server component — loads clip, versions, stamps, splitInto (select id, name). Auth guard (MEMBER redirect + frozen redirect). Maps splitInto and passes to WaveformEditor.
6. app/api/clips/[clipId]/versions/route.ts: POST creates a new version.
7. app/api/clips/[clipId]/split/route.ts: POST { splitMs } — downloads X's source audio once, runs renderAudioWithCuts twice in parallel (Part A: atrim tail, Part B: atrim head), uploads both trimmed files to Drive with distinct names, creates two Clip rows with own driveFileId + correct sourceDurationMs + empty v1 cutMarks. Both have sourceClipId pointing to the original. Original clip is completely unchanged. Returns { clipA: {id, name}, clipB: {id, name} }. Vercel runtime: nodejs, maxDuration: 300.
8. lib/utils.ts: formatPosition(ms) → H:MM:SS always. formatDuration(ms) → M:SS. calcResultDuration(sourceDurationMs, cuts) → virtual duration after removing cut segments.
9. prisma/schema.prisma: Clip.sourceClipId String? (nullable) + SplitFrom self-relation (sourceClip / splitInto) with onDelete: SetNull — deleting a child clip does not cascade to the original.

decisions.decision: WaveformPlayer and WaveformEditor are separate components
decisions.rationale: A single component that handles both playback and editing requires mode-switching logic, conditional overlays, and competing event handlers. Separate components have clear single responsibilities: Player = listen, Editor = cut. The edit page is always in edit mode; no toggle needed. The detail page never needs editing. Each component can be reasoned about and tested independently.
decisions.recommendation: WaveformPlayer lives on clip detail and share pages. WaveformEditor lives exclusively on the /edit page. ClipDetailClient orchestrates state shared between Player and other sections (version pills, collaboration voting).

decisions.decision: interact:false + single overlay div for all pointer handling (no enableDragSelection, no RegionsPlugin)
decisions.rationale: WaveSurfer's enableDragSelection registers a permanent pointer overlay that consumes all pointer events — including click-to-seek. Once called, seeking by clicking is broken. interact:false disables WaveSurfer's built-in event handling entirely. A single overlay div (absolute inset-0, touch-none) inside WaveformCanvas intercepts all pointer events and forwards them to useCutInteraction (trim mode) or useSplitInteraction (split mode).
decisions.recommendation: WaveSurfer({ interact: false }) for all instances. Cut bands as CSS divs with pointer-events:none. No enableDragSelection(), no RegionsPlugin. See feat-timeline-editor for full component breakdown.

decisions.decision: Split produces independent trimmed Drive files immediately (not virtual clips sharing source)
decisions.rationale: Original model: Part A and Part B shared Song X's Drive file and applied cuts virtually — correct waveform, wrong duration in clip list (both showed full 9-min duration instead of 4 min and 5 min). Trimmed files fix duration display without special-casing. They also make Part A and Part B genuinely independent: they can be frozen separately without needing X's file, and X's source can be safely deleted from Drive once A and B are confirmed. FFmpeg at split time costs a few seconds of processing but eliminates a class of edge cases.
decisions.recommendation: Split API: download X once, FFmpeg trim twice in parallel, upload both to Drive, create Clip rows with own driveFileId + correct sourceDurationMs + empty v1 cutMarks. Confirmation copy acknowledges the wait: 'Each part gets its own audio file in Drive. Takes a few seconds — hang tight.'

decisions.decision: No provenance UI on split children
decisions.rationale: Showing scissors icons and a 'Split into' provenance card on Song X implied that Song X was consumed or changed by the split. The correct mental model is: X exists unchanged, A and B are new songs. Their name might hint at their origin (e.g. 'Groove - Part A') but they are first-class independent songs from the moment they're created. Provenance exists in the DB (sourceClipId) for integrity but is not surfaced in the UI.
decisions.recommendation: Remove splitInto prop from WaveformEditor. Remove scissors icon from 'Start from' version buttons. Keep sourceClipId in the DB schema for onDelete: SetNull safety.

decisions.decision: Split navigates to session page, not back to clip detail
decisions.rationale: After a split, three clips exist (original + Part A + Part B). router.back() would return to the original clip's detail — neither Part A nor Part B would be visible. router.push(sessionHref) lands on the session clip list where all three are visible and accessible.
decisions.recommendation: WaveformEditor accepts sessionHref prop. After successful split API call: router.push(sessionHref).

decisions.decision: No version deselect — clicking active version keeps it selected
decisions.rationale: Deselecting a version set selectedVersionId to null, which caused the player to show 'no cuts' (raw audio). This 'null = raw audio' state had no UI label and was visually identical to a version with no cuts. Confusing. Clicking the active version now keeps it selected — the player always reflects a known version.
decisions.recommendation: onChange={() => setSelectedVersionId(v.id)} — no toggle. To hear raw audio, select v1 (which has no cuts unless the clip was split).

decisions.decision: Versions are metadata only — not audio files
decisions.rationale: Audio duplication per version is prohibitively expensive (43MB+ per clip). Versions are cut instructions (cutMarks JSON). Source audio stored once in Drive. Real audio file produced only on freeze.
decisions.recommendation: Postgres clip_versions: {cutMarks: [{startMs, endMs}], resultDurationMs, description, fromVersionId, createdBy}.

decisions.rationale: Full offline sync adds complexity. The real risk is a browser crash mid-edit. localStorage persists cut marks across crashes. Key: odio:draft:{clipId}. Cleared on successful submit.
decisions.recommendation: WaveformEditor writes cutMarks to localStorage on every change. Draft banner shown on reopen. Resume loads draft; Dismiss clears it.

knownLimitations: Split takes a few seconds on the server (FFmpeg download + trim + upload) — the UI shows a loading state on the confirm button but gives no progress indicator
After splitting, the original Song X remains in the session — the band must manually delete it from the Drive page once they've confirmed A and B are good
If two members edit the same version simultaneously they both produce the next version number — band sees two versions at the same level and must choose
Submit requires internet connection — cuts safe in localStorage but cannot be written to Postgres offline
userGuide: From a clip's detail page, select a version in the Versions section to hear that version's cuts applied. Tapping stamp chips (🔥 ⭐ etc.) in the player seeks to that moment. Tap 'Edit cuts' to open the full-screen editor. In the editor: drag on the waveform to create a red cut region; tap Trim to pre-seed start/end cuts; tap Clear all to reset; tap Split to divide the song at a chosen time position — mark the split point on the waveform, confirm, and Odio creates Part A and Part B as two fully independent new songs, each with their own trimmed audio file in Drive. The original song stays in the session unchanged. After confirming Part A and Part B are good, go to Settings → Manage files / sync to delete the original's source from Drive. Tap 'Start from' pills to load a previous version's cuts as a starting point. Tap 'Submit edit' to save as a new version — description is optional.

statusHistory.reason: Major architectural redesign: WaveformPlayer + WaveformEditor split, ClipDetailClient coordinator, version deselect fix, split navigation, click-to-seek fix

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Separated WaveformPlayer (pure play) from WaveformEditor (pure edit). ClipDetailClient owns selectedVersionId. Click-to-seek fixed via pointerEvents:none on regions. Split navigates to session page. Stamp chips are tappable. Version deselect removed.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Non-destructive split: original clip unchanged, creates two independent new clips (Part A + Part B) with sourceClipId provenance. (Note: scissors icon + description on split-origin version buttons and splitInto provenance card were later removed — see subsequent revision.) Scissors icon + description on split-origin version buttons. 'Split into' provenance card on original clip editor. WaveformPlayer finish event resets cursor to start. timeupdate guard scope fix (position tracking unconditional, isPlaying gate wraps only cut-skip section). Schema: sourceClipId self-relation added to Clip.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Split redesign: FFmpeg trims independent Drive files at split time (not virtual clips sharing source). Correct durations shown in clip list (latestResultDurationMs). No provenance UI on split children — fully independent songs. splitInto prop removed from WaveformEditor. HEAD 502 fix: explicit HEAD handler for /api/audio/[clipId] uses getDriveFileMeta (no streaming).

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Corrected stale waveform architecture: interact:false (not true), RegionsPlugin removed (CSS divs via WaveformCanvas), useCutInteraction handles drag/resize. Removed stale limitation about edge handles blocking resize. Merged duplicate pointerEvents:none decision into single interact:false decision.
